body_txt: Due to how devastating and terrifying tornadoes and other wind-based natural disasters can be in real life, it can be easy to anthropomorphize them, imparting malicious intent to them. Some works of fiction do just that and have whirlwinds that are conscious and capable of directing their own movement. Unlike real tornadoes, these characters will tend to only suck up or blow away things they directly touch . If they show up as an enemy in a video game, they're likely to use wind-based attacks . Some of these characters may have a body or body parts blowing around inside the whirlwind, but they can still count as long as the whirlwind is a primary part of their body. When humanoid, they tend to have Fog Feet . Due to the aforementioned associations of whirlwinds with destruction, living tornadoes in fiction are likely to be antagonistic and chaotic. Some may even be portrayed as unstoppable if they're large enough. Sub-Trope of Elemental Embodiment . Compare Cumulonemesis , Winds Are Ghosts , Hostile Weather , The Stormbringer , and The Old North Wind . Not to be confused with Tornado Move ; that trope describes characters who merely temporarily create a tornado for an attack, instead of being a living tornado themselves. Can overlap with Elemental Giant if the whirlwind is of sufficient enough size.
